mnemonics.title: TM Components Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: Think of TM as 'T-RAP HQS': Tape, Read/Write head, Alphabet (tape symbols), Program (transition function), Head movement (L/R), Q (states), Start state. The order spells: T-RAP HQS helps remember the 5 core components.

mnemonics.title: Church-Turing Thesis
mnemonics.content: 'Church Said Turing Can Compute Anything Algorithmic' - Church and Turing together established that Turing Machines capture all algorithmic computation.

mnemonics.title: Decidable vs RE
mnemonics.content: DECIDABLE = 'Definitely Ends Computation In Deciding All Inputs' (halts on all). RE = 'Recognizes/Enumerates but may run forever on rejections'.

acronyms.usage: The abstract computational model with infinite tape, read/write head, and finite control.
TM where each state-symbol pair has at most one transition.
TM where each state-symbol pair can have multiple possible transitions.
A TM that can simulate any other TM - the concept of stored-program computer.
Languages for which a TM exists that accepts all strings in the language.
Languages for which a TM always halts and correctly accepts/rejects.
The set of symbols that can appear in the input string (excludes blank).
The complete set of symbols that can appear on tape (includes Σ and blank B).

visualTips.visualization: Picture a long cassette tape with a small window (read/write head) that can slide left/right. The tape has infinite cells in both directions. The 'control unit' is like a small CPU reading instructions based on current state and symbol seen.

Think of a railroad switch: At position (current state, current symbol), the TM decides: what to write (swap tracks), which way to go (left/right track), and which new state to enter (destination station).
Imagine all possible algorithms as different-shaped keys. The Turing Machine is the 'master lock' - if any key (algorithm) can open a problem, the TM key can too.
Imagine a program that analyzes other programs - it would need to predict its own future behavior, creating an infinite loop of self-reference, making it impossible to decide universally.

analogies.analogy: Like an infinitely long roll of paper tape in a telegraph machine - you can always keep moving to read or write more information. Unlike human memory (finite), this provides unbounded storage.
Like a programmable universal machine - it can become any specific machine by reading the description (program) of that machine, just like how modern computers run different software.
FA is like a vending machine with fixed buttons (limited states). TM is like having an unlimited notepad to write down calculations and refer back to them anytime.
Like a forever-running search that finds all matches but may never finish searching non-matches - you'll eventually see any correct answer but may wait forever for a rejection.
